name: response_quality
description: Verifies that responses are useful and concise

tests.description: Responses should be <= 200 words (target 150, margin for LLM variability)
tests.input: Tell me absolutely everything about yourself, all your experience, projects, skills, education and certifications

tests.description: Should not use markdown lists with - or 1.
Should include concrete metrics when talking about achievements
tests.input: What did you achieve at your company?

tests.description: Responses should not be too short
tests.input: What is your experience with AI?

tests.description: Tone should be professional but approachable, not robotic

assertions.criteria: The tone is professional but approachable, conveys confidence without being arrogant, and feels authentic (not like a generic chatbot)

tests.description: The second response to the same question should vary
tests.input: What is your experience with AI?

conversation.content: What is your experience with AI?

I've been building with AI in production for years. At Santifer iRepair I created Jacobo, an omnichannel agent (voice + WhatsApp) that resolved 90% of queries without human intervention. It orchestrated specialized sub-agents with tool calling and HITL handoff. I also built Custom GPTs with OpenAPI for voice-based stock queries. Now with this portfolio I apply real LLMOps: Langfuse, 31 automated evals, prompt caching. Want me to dig deeper into a specific project?
What is your experience with AI?

assertions.criteria: The response provides new information or a different angle on AI experience, not repeating exactly the same points (Jacobo, 90%, tool calling) from the previous response included in the conversation context

tests.description: For a generic question, should give a concrete answer and suggest digging deeper
